result:
I now have enough data from both the wiki and source to produce the full inventory. Here is the complete report.

---

## oCMS Cache Layer Inventory

### Starting point

Read `../llm-wiki-go/wiki/topics/caching.md` → `sources/docs-caching.md` → `sources/wiki-caching.md` first. Both source pages agree with the topic page; the wiki source comparison note reads: *"No factual disagreements."* No `## Contradictions` block exists in any of the three pages.

Source fallbacks (listed in **§ Wiki gaps** below) were used only where the wiki genuinely lacked the fact.

---

### Layer 1 — Config cache

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/` — `config.go`, `manager.go` |
| **Env vars / defaults** | None governing TTL — see §7 for `OCMS_CACHE_TTL` correction |
| **TTL default** | **1 hour** (hardcoded: `New(time.Hour)` in `NewConfigCache`) |
| **What invalidates it** | `m.Config.Invalidate()` — called on any site-config save; also via `InvalidateThemeSettings()` (theme settings share the config store) |
| **Consumers** | `middleware.LoadSiteConfig(db, cacheManager)` on frontend routes only. Admin routes pass `nil` as the manager and bypass this cache entirely. |

Source: `wiki/topics/caching.md`; admin-bypass wiring confirmed at `cmd/ocms/main.go:738–751` (wiki gap #1 — TTL is hardcoded, not via env var).

---

### Layer 2 — Sitemap cache

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/sitemap.go`, `manager.go` |
| **Env vars / defaults** | None; sitemap URL must be set in site config (`site_url`) or preload skips it |
| **TTL default** | **1 hour** (hardcoded: `NewSitemapCache(queries, time.Hour)` in `NewManager`) |
| **What invalidates it** | `m.Sitemap.Invalidate()` — called by `InvalidateContent()`, `InvalidatePage(id)`, `InvalidatePages()` on page/category/tag create-update-delete |
| **Consumers** | SEO subsystem — `sitemap.xml` handler |

Source: `wiki/topics/caching.md`; TTL confirmation: `sitemap.go:31–39` (wiki gap — hardcoded, not `OCMS_CACHE_TTL`).

---

### Layer 3 — Page cache

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/pages.go`, `manager.go` |
| **Env vars / defaults** | None for in-memory TTL |
| **TTL default** | **1 hour** (hardcoded: `New(time.Hour)` in `NewPageCache`) |
| **Cache key shape** | `{language}:{role}:{slug}` and `{language}:{role}:{id}` — role levels: `anonymous`/`public` = 0, `editor` = 1, `admin` = 2 |
| **What invalidates it** | `m.Page.Invalidate()` (all) or `m.Page.InvalidatePage(id)` (single page); triggered on page create / update / delete / publish-toggle (0.18.1 fix) |
| **Consumers** | Frontend theme rendering — published-page lookup by slug or ID |

Source: `wiki/topics/caching.md`; role levels confirmed; TTL source gap at `pages.go:33–41`.

---

### Layer 4 — Menu cache

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/menus.go`, `manager.go` |
| **Env vars / defaults** | None |
| **TTL default** | **1 hour** (hardcoded: `New(time.Hour)` in `NewMenuCache`) |
| **What invalidates it** | `m.Menus.Invalidate()` — called on menu or menu-item create / update / delete |
| **Consumers** | Frontend theme rendering — navigation menus per language |

Source: `wiki/topics/caching.md`; TTL confirmation: `menus.go:34–39`.

---

### Layer 5 — Language cache

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/languages.go`, `manager.go` |
| **Env vars / defaults** | None |
| **TTL default** | **1 hour** (hardcoded: `New(time.Hour)` in `NewLanguageCache`) |
| **What invalidates it** | `m.Language.Invalidate()` — called on language create / update / delete |
| **Consumers** | i18n subsystem, middleware (language detection), API (language resolution), preloaded at startup |

Source: `wiki/topics/caching.md`; TTL confirmation: `languages.go:29–35`.

---

### Layer 6 — Translation cache

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/translations.go`, `manager.go` |
| **Env vars / defaults** | None |
| **TTL default** | **30 minutes** (hardcoded: `New(30 * time.Minute)` in `NewTranslationCache`) — max 10,000 entries |
| **What invalidates it** | `m.Translation.Invalidate()` (all) or `m.Translation.InvalidateEntity(entityType, entityID)` (single entity); called on translation create / update / delete |
| **Consumers** | Multi-language content serving; translation-link lookups during page rendering |

Source: `wiki/topics/caching.md` (type listed but TTL not specified); TTL confirmed at `translations.go:39–47` — **this differs from all other domain caches** (wiki gap #2).

---

### Layer 7 — General / miscellaneous cache

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/manager.go` field `General *SimpleCache` |
| **Env vars / defaults** | None |
| **TTL default** | **5 minutes** (hardcoded: `New(5 * time.Minute)`) |
| **What invalidates it** | `m.General.Clear()` — called only by `ClearAll()`; no targeted invalidation |
| **Consumers** | None found in production code — declared and lifecycle-managed (start/stop/stats/clear) but no `Get`/`Set` calls exist outside tests. Reserved capacity for future miscellaneous caching. |

Source: `manager.go:71–72` (wiki gap #3 — not mentioned in the wiki at all).

---

### Layer 8 — Distributed / Redis backing cache

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/redis.go`, `factory.go`, `memory.go`; wired in `manager.go` field `Distributed Cache` |
| **Env vars / defaults** | `OCMS_REDIS_URL` (unset = memory-only); `OCMS_CACHE_PREFIX` = `ocms:`; `OCMS_CACHE_TTL` = `3600` s; `OCMS_CACHE_MAX_SIZE` = `10000`; `FallbackToMemory = true` |
| **TTL default** | **3600 s** (`OCMS_CACHE_TTL`) — this is the `DefaultTTL` for the Redis backend, not for the in-memory domain caches |
| **What invalidates it** | `m.Distributed.Clear(ctx)` — only called by `ClearAll()`. No per-key invalidation paths exist for the distributed layer currently. |
| **Consumers** | Exists as a `Cache` interface implementor that domain caches could delegate to, but the domain caches (`ConfigCache`, `PageCache`, etc.) each own a private `*SimpleCache` and do not delegate to `Distributed` at runtime. `Distributed` is wired for future use / health-check surface. |

Source: `wiki/topics/caching.md` (backends section); confirmed at `cmd/ocms/main.go:732–762` and `manager.go:82–100`.

> **Note:** The wiki states *"Default TTL for entries: `OCMS_CACHE_TTL` (default `3600` seconds)"* without qualification. In practice, `OCMS_CACHE_TTL` is passed only to the Redis/distributed backend's `DefaultTTL`. All six in-memory domain caches (`Config`, `Sitemap`, `Page`, `Menu`, `Language`, `Translation`) have their own hardcoded TTLs and do not read `OCMS_CACHE_TTL`.

---

### Layer 9 — API rate-limiter cache

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Package / file** | `internal/middleware/api.go` — `limiterCache[string]` (generic type) |
| **Env vars / defaults** | `OCMS_API_RATE_LIMIT` = `100` req/min; no env var for TTL |
| **TTL default** | **10-minute idle eviction** (`limiterCacheTTL = 10 * time.Minute`); cleanup goroutine runs every 5 min (`limiterCacheCleanupInterval`); hard cap at **10,000** entries (`limiterCacheMaxEntries`) with LRU eviction |
| **What invalidates it** | Time-based idle eviction only; no explicit API-triggered invalidation |
| **Consumers** | `middleware.APIRateLimit` — per-API-key token-bucket enforcement on `POST /api/v2/*` and other rate-limited REST routes |

Source: `api.go:923–1007` (wiki gap #4 — not mentioned in the wiki).

---

### Layer 10 — Login IP rate-limiter cache

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Package / file** | `internal/middleware/login_protection.go` field `ipLimiters *limiterCache[string]`; same `limiterCache[K]` type from `api.go` |
| **Env vars / defaults** | No env vars; configured programmatically: default **0.5 RPS** / burst **5** (`DefaultLoginProtectionConfig`) |
| **TTL default** | Inherits `limiterCacheTTL = 10 min` idle eviction and `limiterCacheMaxEntries = 10,000` cap from `api.go` |
| **What invalidates it** | Idle eviction only; IP-level state is intentionally in-memory and lost on restart (account-lockout state is persisted to SQLite separately) |
| **Consumers** | Login form handler — IP-level brute-force rate limiting; `LoginProtection.Allow(ip)` |

Source: `login_protection.go:36–43` and `api.go:923–932` (wiki gap #5 — not mentioned in the wiki).

---

## Contradictions

None. The wiki source comparison explicitly states *"No factual disagreements"* between `docs/caching.md` and `wiki/Caching.md`. No `## Contradictions` block exists on any wiki page consulted.

---

## Wiki gaps

The following facts required falling back to oCMS source because the wiki does not cover them:

| # | Gap | Source location |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | All in-memory domain cache TTLs are **hardcoded** in their constructors, not controlled by `OCMS_CACHE_TTL`. The wiki implies the env var is the global TTL. | `config.go:29–35`, `pages.go:33–41`, `menus.go:34–39`, `languages.go:29–35`, `sitemap.go:31–39` |
| 2 | **Translation cache TTL is 30 min**, not 1 hour (like other domain caches). | `translations.go:39–47` |
| 3 | **`General` cache** (`SimpleCache`, 5-min TTL) exists in the Manager but is unmentioned in the wiki and has no production consumers today. | `manager.go:71–72`, `manager.go:122–189` |
| 4 | **API rate-limiter cache** (`limiterCache[string]`, 10-min idle TTL, 10 k max, `OCMS_API_RATE_LIMIT` env var) is entirely absent from the wiki. | `api.go:923–1007` |
| 5 | **Login IP rate-limiter cache** (`limiterCache[string]` inside `LoginProtection`, 0.5 RPS / burst 5) is absent from the wiki. | `login_protection.go:36–43` |
| 6 | `OCMS_CACHE_TTL` and `OCMS_CACHE_MAX_SIZE` are passed only to the **Redis/distributed backend** config; the Distributed cache is wired but domain caches do not delegate to it at runtime. | `cmd/ocms/main.go:734–746`, `manager.go:82–100` |
